---
# Source Card: WHO Child Growth Standards: Length/height-for-age

## Summary
The WHO length/height-for-age standard (2006) is a prescriptive international growth standard covering birth to 5 years, split into recumbent length-for-age (birth to 2 years) and standing height-for-age (2 to 5 years), issued as z-score and percentile charts and tables for boys and girls. For a CDS module the operational rules are the load-bearing content: which indicator applies is governed by an exact age boundary at 24 months / 731 days, recumbent length and standing height differ by a modelled 0.7 cm (subtract from length to get height, add to height to get length), and biologically-implausible-value bounds gate the computation. These operational thresholds are quoted verbatim from the companion WHO Anthro for Personal Computers Manual v3.2.2, which operationalizes the same standards.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Indicator by age: length < 24 mo, height >= 24 mo — locator: Anthro Manual v3.2.2 §3.2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:266d6672dd068f47f24b9ce5ab195451d57e8b3e6c17493028e68a950a2cd12a]"
- (ev_002) Exact day boundary: <=730 d length-for-age, >=731 d height-for-age — locator: Anthro Manual v3.2.2 §3.3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a8fd24852354bb8378c9243e48f52fadd671ffe6b39b3aeac34a01e80af330f0]"
- (ev_003) 0.7 cm method conversion (length<->height) — locator: Anthro Manual v3.2.2 §3.3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:1a3066d206520ef61e0ed682dd4cfc1ea15d19e0dd4e737112d7489e3342646f]"
- (ev_004) Basis of 0.7 cm: MGRS 18-30 mo paired measurements — locator: Anthro Manual v3.2.2 §3.3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:32464d93fc018b84190e53cd0f832a929bc01fb94cea89e52476e4ccaed29bb2]"
- (ev_005) 87 cm fallback cutoff / medians at 24 mo — locator: Anthro Manual v3.2.2 §3.3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:40369106fd7bd5f8fc89376427e4d901c4394dbec72f59792033bc985a3b6415]"
- (ev_006) Implausible-value bounds: length 45-110 cm (<24 mo), height 65-120 cm (>=24 mo) — locator: Anthro Manual v3.2.2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:bdcd95ad0a084ab32e3e8ce33d811af520cc561ee0bb197ebe8f75fbabb4cc94]"
- (ev_007) Artifact set: length birth-2 y, height 2-5 y, combined birth-5 y, z-scores + percentiles, boys & girls — locator: WHO standards page index.

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: healthy-growth WHO MGRS reference; standard spans birth to 5 years, both sexes, sex-specific.
- Assay/method dependence is central: recumbent length (board) below 24 mo vs standing height (stadiometer) at/above 24 mo are NOT interchangeable — the modelled 0.7 cm offset reconciles a position/age mismatch before any indicator is derived. A CDS must capture measurement position, not just the number.
- Lifecycle: 2006 standards; operational thresholds from Anthro v3.2.2 (2011). No retirement date; these remain the current WHO standards as of 2026-07.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The biologically-implausible bounds (ev_006: length 45-110 cm under 24 mo; height 65-120 cm at/over 24 mo) are data-quality exclusion gates that flag likely measurement/entry errors and set indicators to NA — a value outside these ranges is a "do not compute / re-measure" signal, not a clinical z-score. This is an input-validation scope boundary, NOT a diagnosis or referral threshold. This card defines no red-flag, treatment, or referral trigger.

## Limitations & conflicts
- The standards page itself carries prose only as chart/table labels; all prose numeric thresholds are quoted from the companion WHO Anthro for PC Manual v3.2.2 (attributed per point). Threshold provenance is honest but cross-document.
- CONFLICT (preserved, not collapsed): WHO standards are prescriptive international standards (optimal growth); the CDC 2000 growth charts are a descriptive US reference. CDC recommends WHO standards for ages 0-24 months and CDC charts for children >=2 years in US clinical use. The two reference systems are not averaged or merged here; a CDS choosing a reference must select one system explicitly per age/population.
- The reported average length-height difference of ~0.73 cm cited in some WHO methods documents was NOT locatable with a verbatim passage in the source read here (only the rounded 0.7 cm operational value was quotable) — recorded as a GAP; do not assert 0.73 cm from this card.
- Sex-specific and gestational/preterm handling: WHO Child Growth Standards are for term-born children and use postnatal (chronological) age; they do not provide corrected-age adjustment for preterm infants. Not addressed on this page — do not infer.
